meta.title: Zhejiang
meta.subtitle: Industry-cluster overview
meta.tagline: Zhejiang is China's 'one town, one product' taken to its limit — a single county might make a third of the world's socks, or most of its pearls. This is the cluster map of the province that perfected the block economy.
meta.methodology: Coordinates are from Google Places, converted from China's GCJ-02 datum to WGS-84 to sit correctly on the OpenStreetMap / CARTO basemap. Each pin marks a place's dominant manufacturing specialty drawn from current sourcing guides; clusters are indicative, not exclusive, and shift over time. The map covers Zhejiang's eleven prefecture-level cities; the Yangtze River Delta overview maps Zhejiang within its wider region.

clusters.note: E-commerce and tech capital (Alibaba); also the historic Hangzhou silk industry.
China's pen-making capital; also medical devices.
Menswear (Youngor, Shanshan), auto parts and injection moulds; a top global port.
Small home-appliance capital; plastics and connectors.
Plastics City — moulds, injection parts and connectors.
Mould-making and stationery; lighting.
Leather-shoe capital and the world's metal-lighter capital; eyewear, pens and locks.
Low-voltage electrical-apparatus capital — Chint and Delixi.
China Textile City — the world's largest fabric market; printing and dyeing.
Sock capital — about a third of the world's socks.
Freshwater-pearl capital — most of the world's freshwater pearls.
Necktie capital — about a third of the world's ties; also kitchen appliances.
The world's small-commodity capital.
Hardware capital — hand and power tools, pans, security doors, electric scooters.
Woodcarving and classical furniture; Hengdian magnetics and film studios.
Crystal and glass-crafts capital; padlocks.
Door locks, hand tools and insulated cups and thermos flasks.
Mould capital — plastic moulds and plastics; pharma APIs nearby.
Water-pump and motor capital; valves and shoes.
Geely's birthplace — autos, motorcycles and recycled metals.
China Leather City — leather garments; warp-knit and home textiles.
Sweater and cashmere-knit capital.
Export-garment base; optoelectronics and case-making.
China's children's-wear capital.
Office-chair capital; bamboo flooring and products.
Wood-flooring capital; elevators.
Lead-acid battery base (Tianneng, Chaowei).
Fluorine and silicon chemicals; paper and new materials.
Wooden-toy capital; Qingtian stone carving and synthetic leather nearby.
China's great fishing port — seafood processing and shipbuilding.

orientation.v: Zhejiang perfected 'one town, one product' — villages each owning a single global niche, from socks to ties to pearls.
Private-enterprise heartland: Wenzhou shoes, lighters and Liushi electrical; Taizhou moulds, pumps and Geely autos.
Keqiao fabric, Datang socks, Shanxiahu pearls, Shengzhou ties; Yiwu commodities, Yongkang hardware, Dongyang woodcarving.
Ningbo menswear and moulds; Cixi appliances; Jiaxing leather (Haining) and sweaters (Puyuan); Huzhou kids' wear and chairs.
Two of the world's great markets sit here — Yiwu for small commodities and Keqiao for fabric.

notes: Zhejiang is the purest expression of China's block economy — clusters so specialised that single towns dominate a global product: a third of the world's socks (Datang), most of its freshwater pearls (Shanxiahu), a third of its neckties (Shengzhou).
It splits into a private-enterprise south (Wenzhou shoes and electrical, Taizhou moulds, pumps and autos) and a textile-and-trade north around Hangzhou Bay (Ningbo, Shaoxing, Jiaxing, Huzhou).
Two of the world's largest wholesale markets anchor the province — Yiwu for small commodities and Keqiao for fabric — both buyer destinations, not just production bases.

A pin marks a place's dominant specialty, not its only one; many of these towns make several related products. The Yangtze River Delta overview map covers Zhejiang alongside Jiangsu and Shanghai; this map zooms in on the province.
